concerns.text: Task T004b is marked as [ ] (incomplete) and references FR-007 for seed injection into data/run_log.json. However, the spec's FR-007 requires documenting seeds in a reproducible configuration file. The task description implies a verification step against config.yaml which is currently missing (per analyze report), leaving the requirement for seed reproducibility and verification unimplemented.

Task T018a is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It is critical for FR-001 (global batch generation) and SC-001 (success rate). The task implements the 10-attempt retry loop and global batch aggregation. Without this task, the system cannot satisfy the requirement to generate a sufficient volume of valid connected graphs or handle the 'Disconnected Networks' edge case as specified.

Tasks T029a (schema definition) and T029 (serialization) are marked as [ ] (incomplete) and referenced as failed. These are required for FR-003 (measuring diffusion rate) and FR-002 (simulation output). The 'SimulationRun' entity defined in the spec has no corresponding implementation task to persist its attributes (network_id, seed, diffusion_rate, etc.) to data/analysis/simulation_results.json.

Task T035 (sensitivity sweep) is marked as [ ] (incomplete). This is the sole task covering FR-008 (sensitivity analysis on clustering thresholds) and SC-005 (reporting at ≥5 distinct cutoffs). Without this task, the system cannot perform the required sensitivity analysis or verify the robustness of correlation findings.

Task T037 (master analysis script) is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It is the aggregation point for US-3, depending on T035 and T029. Without this task, there is no mechanism to run the regression/ANOVA tests (T033, T034), generate figures (T036), or produce the final results file (data/analysis/final_results.json) required for the research output.

Task T044 (power analysis) is marked as [ ] (incomplete). This task is required to satisfy SC-003 (statistical power measured against design target) and FR-001 (sufficient scale). The task calculates achieved power and generates a validation report. Its absence leaves the statistical validity of the sample size unverified.

Task T045 (batch validation script) is marked as [ ] (incomplete). This task is required to verify SC-001 (success rate), SC-002 (runtime), and SC-005 (sensitivity cutoffs). The analyze report indicates this task is in a re-plan loop due to missing artifacts, but the task itself remains unimplemented, leaving the success criteria unverified.

Task T050 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It addresses the handling of missing or divergent simulation runs (Edge Case: Simulation Divergence) in the analysis pipeline. Without this, the system may include corrupted data in statistical aggregations, violating the data hygiene and statistical integrity principles.

Task T051 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It refactors the simulation runner to strictly enforce the 10-attempt retry logic for disconnected networks (Edge Case). Without this, the system may fail to generate the required volume of valid graphs or incorrectly halt the batch, violating FR-001 and the Edge Case specification.

Task T052 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It adds explicit power analysis validation to T044. Without this, the system cannot verify if the generated sample size meets the design target for detecting effect size r ≥ 0.3, leaving SC-003 unfulfilled.

Task T053 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It enhances the batch aggregation to check for 'sufficient scale' (FR-001) and adjust target batch sizes. Without this, the system may fail to generate enough data for subgroup ANOVA tests, violating the statistical power requirements.

Task T054 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It implements a strict numerical stability guard for simulation divergence (Edge Case). Without this, the system may produce corrupted data that skews results, violating the stability requirements of FR-002 and the Edge Case specification.

Task T055 is marked as [ ] (incomplete). It creates a comprehensive validation script to verify sensitivity sweep integrity (SC-005). Without this, the system cannot ensure the sensitivity analysis meets the requirement of ≥5 distinct cutoffs.
